title: Record pilot episode 1 with Brian Mitchell on team dynamics
description: Record the first pilot in the basement studio on the Shure MV7. Topic: what the research actually says about team dynamics versus the folklore. Keep it to a 30-minute cut.

title: Record pilot episode 2 on psychological safety as a measurable driver
description: Solo framing episode that mirrors the book thesis: psychological safety is measurable and it moves business outcomes. Use it as the anchor for the trailer.

title: Record pilot episode 3 with Patricia Okafor on coaching for candor
description: Conversation with Patricia Okafor on how executive coaching builds the candor a team needs. Recorded remotely; confirm her levels before we start.

title: Record pilot episode 4 on the cost of silence in clinical teams
description: Draw on the culture work with healthcare clients: what unspoken concerns cost when nobody names them. Keep specifics anonymized.

title: Edit pilot 1 in PodBean Pro and tighten to 30 minutes
description: Cut the Brian Mitchell pilot to a clean 30-minute runtime. Remove the two false starts, level the room tone, and add the standard cold open.

title: Edit pilot 2 and master the intro and outro beds
description: Editing pass on the solo safety episode plus a reusable intro and outro music bed for the whole season. Export the beds separately so we do not re-cut them each week.

title: Book three season-one guests from the coaching community
description: Reach out through the leadership-coaching Discord and LinkedIn to line up three credible guests for the opening run. Priya to hold calendar slots once confirmed.

title: Design show artwork and cover image for Leading Out Loud
description: Build the cover art in Canva Pro at 3000x3000 for the directories. Keep it clean and readable at thumbnail size; the podcast name has to survive shrinking.

title: Write show notes template and per-episode notes for pilots
description: Standard show-notes template with guest bio, three takeaways, and links. Draft the notes for all four pilots so publishing is a copy-paste job later.

title: Set up PodBean Pro hosting and distribution feeds
description: Configure the PodBean Pro show, verify the RSS feed, and submit to Spotify and Apple. Confirm the $300/mo plan covers the download volume we expect.

title: Cut a 90-second season trailer from the pilots
description: Pull the strongest lines from the four pilots into a trailer that states the premise and invites subscribers. This is the first thing new listeners hear.

title: Plan the weekly publishing cadence and buffer
description: Decide the publish day and build a two-episode buffer before launch so a busy travel week does not break the streak. Reflect it in the calendar with Priya.

title: Acoustic treatment pass on the basement podcast corner
description: Reposition the acoustic panels behind the mic and retest for reflections. The pilot 3 remote call exposed a slight slap echo on my side.

title: Drop the paid ad-read idea for season one
description: Explored a mid-roll sponsor slot but it is premature before we have download numbers. Shelving until after launch.

title: Draft the book proposal overview and hook
description: Two-page overview framing The Safety Equation: psychological safety as a measurable business driver, not a soft nicety. This is what Rick reads first, so it has to land.

title: Draft chapter 1 on why safety is measurable
description: Opening chapter that defines the construct and shows it can be measured with instruments a leader actually trusts. Lean on the Hawthorne and Vantage evidence, anonymized.

title: Draft chapter 2 on the cost of unspoken concerns
description: Chapter connecting silence to measurable outcomes: missed errors, slower decisions, attrition. Keep the argument evidence-first and skip the buzzwords.

title: Assemble comparable titles and market analysis
description: Comp-titles section: where The Safety Equation sits next to the existing shelf and what it adds. Include recent sales context so the case is honest, not inflated.

title: Write the chapter-by-chapter outline for the proposal
description: Full annotated table of contents with a paragraph per chapter. Rick wants to see the whole arc, not just the strong opening.

title: Send proposal package to Rick Halloran and log follow-up
description: Compile the overview, the first chapter, comps, and outline into one package for Rick. Schedule the follow-up call so it does not drift into a black hole.

title: Get a research read from Brian Mitchell on chapter 1
description: Ask Brian to check the measurement claims in chapter 1 against the literature before it goes to the agent. He catches the overreach I miss.

title: Set up manuscript tracking toward the 2027-03-01 target
description: Lay out the drafting schedule that gets the full manuscript done by the March target without blowing up the coaching calendar. Backwards-plan from the deadline.
